CheckTitle: AI Agent Container Must Have Resource Limits Configured
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Runtime Security**. Verifies that every AI agent container has CPU, memory, and disk resource limits configured. Without limits, a runaway or adversarially triggered agent can exhaust shared infrastructure resources and cause a denial-of-service against sibling workloads on the same node.
Risk: An agent without resource limits can consume all available CPU and memory on a node, starving other workloads and potentially causing node-level failures. Adversarial inputs (e.g., prompt injection triggering expensive LLM calls in a loop) can weaponise this into a targeted DoS.

Code.Other:
1. Set resources.limits.cpu and resources.limits.memory in each container spec.
2. Set resources.requests to match expected usage so the scheduler places the pod correctly.
3. For disk, use ephemeral-storage limits or mount a size-limited PersistentVolume.
4. Enable LimitRange objects in the namespace to enforce defaults cluster-wide.

Recommendation.Text: Configure CPU, memory, and ephemeral storage limits for every agent container. Use LimitRange and ResourceQuota objects to enforce limits namespace-wide and prevent regressions.

Notes: ASPM-059 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
